codeReview.summary: Fail. The fix closes the text duplicate case from the prior review, but the current `session_input_callback` provenance matcher still cannot preserve identity for duplicate-valued `InputItem::Json` objects, numbers, booleans, or nulls. For those items the code falls back to history-first frequency matching, so a callback that returns the new-turn copy of a duplicate JSON item is still attributed to history instead of the new turn.

issues.description: `json_value_identity()` returns `None` for `Value::Object`, `Value::Number`, `Value::Bool`, and `Value::Null`, so duplicate-valued JSON inputs never participate in the new-vs-history identity match. In the main classification loop, those items therefore hit the history-first frequency fallback (`history_counts` before `new_counts`) and can still be misattributed to history when the callback actually returned the new-turn item. Upstream Python/JS use reference identity for the copied callback pools before frequency fallback, so the current Rust fix is still incomplete for non-string/non-array JSON input items.

summary: Reviewed the fix transcript skeleton, both feature diffs, the handoffs, the current repository state, and the shared-state guidance. The new commit improves duplicate-valued text handling and adds a focused text regression, but the current `session_persistence` implementation still loses provenance for duplicate-valued JSON inputs whose identity is not tracked, so the original blocker is not fully resolved yet.
